summary: Verified Project Templates gallery, Netlify deploy integration, batch monitor check endpoint, and OOM memory fix. All 11 backend tests passed. Frontend UI verified: New Project modal shows 6 template options (Blank + 5 templates), Deploy tab shows 3 cards (Download ZIP, Vercel, Netlify) with 'Connect Netlify' button, Share button visible in RightPanel toolbar.

templates_api.GET_templates_returns_5: PASS - returns 5 templates with correct structure
templates_api.templates_have_correct_fields: PASS - id, name, description, category present
templates_api.templates_have_categories: PASS - Marketing, Personal, Business, Content, Commerce

project_creation_with_template.creates_with_template: PASS - creates project and populates files from template

netlify_deploy.requires_token: PASS - returns 400 'Netlify token is required' without token

critical_code_review_comments: GET /api/templates correctly returns 5 templates with id, name, description, category, icon, color, file_count
POST /api/projects with template_id correctly populates project files from template
POST /api/projects/:id/deploy/netlify correctly returns 400 when token is missing
POST /api/growth/monitors/check-all correctly requires auth and performs batch check
Supervisor config has NODE_OPTIONS='--max-old-space-size=2048' for 2GB heap
next.config.js has filesystem cache enabled for webpack
Note: 'memory threshold' warnings still appear in logs - this is Next.js dev server behavior, not a bug
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_Deploy_Tab_Project with Landing Page template for testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 60 verified Project Templates gallery (6 options: Blank + 5 templates), Netlify deploy integration (requires token, returns 400 without), batch monitor check endpoint (POST /api/growth/monitors/check-all), and OOM memory fix (2GB heap in supervisor config). Deploy tab shows 3 cards: Download ZIP, Vercel, Netlify. Share button is in RightPanel toolbar. Memory threshold warnings still appear in logs but this is expected Next.js dev server behavior.
oom_memory_note: The 'memory threshold' warnings still appear in server logs. This is expected behavior from Next.js dev server's internal memory monitoring. The fix (2GB heap via NODE_OPTIONS) is correctly applied in supervisor config. The warnings are informational and do not indicate a problem - the server auto-restarts to prevent actual OOM crashes.
